title: quercus macranthera subsp. syspirensis
intro: Quercus macranthera subsp. syspirensis, also known as the Sipylos oak, is a subspecies of evergreen oak native to the mountainous regions of Turkey. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Quercus macranthera subsp. syspirensis include potential anti-cancer and anti-diabetic effects, as well as cardiovascular protection and immune system support. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing its leaves in hot water or oil, or by extracting its bioactive compounds using solvents, to create teas, tinctures, and ointments for topical use.
